_comment: English → Japanese | Month 1 Week 2 | Lesson — Numbers & Colors
title: Numbers, Colors, and Daily Objects
description: Learn to count 1-100 in Japanese, name basic colors, and identify everyday items using Core Hiragana.

blocks.title: Counting in Japanese: The Magic of Numbers
blocks.body:
Numbers (数字 - Suuji) in Japanese are very logical!

1: いち (ichi)
2: に (ni)
3: さん (san)
4: よん (yon) / し (shi)
5: ご (go)
6: ろく (roku)
7: なな (nana) / しち (shichi)
8: はち (hachi)
9: きゅう (kyuu)
10: じゅう (juu)

After 10, it's just math!
11 is 'ten-one' (じゅういち - juu-ichi)
20 is 'two-tens' (にじゅう - ni-juu)
21 is 'two-tens-one' (にじゅういち - ni-juu-ichi)
This simple pattern continues all the way to 99!

blocks.title: This Week's Focus
points: Asking 'How much is it?' (いくらですか - Ikura desu ka)

blocks.title: Kore, Sore, Are (これ、それ、あれ)

examples.native: That over there is a car.
examples.target: あれは くるまです。
examples.translation: Are wa kuruma desu. (Far from both)

blocks.note: English has 'this' and 'that'. Japanese has THREE words: Kore (near me), Sore (near you), and Are (far from both of us).

lines.text: すみません、これは いくらですか？
lines.romanization: Sumimasen, kore wa ikura desu ka?
lines.translation: Excuse me, how much is this?

lines.text: それは ひゃくえんです。
じゃあ、これを ください。
lines.translation: Well then, I'll take this please.

lines.text: ありがとうございます！

blocks.body: 💡 Pro tip: Japanese currency is Yen, but it's pronounced 'En' (えん) in Japanese. 100 Yen is 'Hyaku En' (ひゃくえん).

blocks.title: Number Check
blocks.instructions: Fill in the correct Japanese number (Romaji):

items.sentence: The color of an apple is ___ (red).

blocks.title: Lucky and Unlucky Numbers
blocks.content: In Japan, the numbers 4 and 9 are considered unlucky. 4 is pronounced 'shi', which sounds exactly like the word for death (死). 9 is pronounced 'ku', which sounds like the word for suffering (苦). Hospitals and hotels often skip rooms 4 and 9, and gifts shouldn't be given in sets of 4!

blocks.title: Week 2 Checkpoint
items: Ask for prices using 'ikura desu ka'
